heading: Beneficial ownership information reporting requirements
label: 5336 Beneficial ownership information reporting requirements
notes: References in TextThe Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (a)(7), (11)(B)(xix), (14), is classified generally to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code. Sections 501(a), (c), 508(a), 527(a), (e)(1), 4947(a), and 7701(a) are classified to sections 501(a), (c), 508(a), 527(a), (e)(1), 4947(a), and 7701(a), respectively, of Title 26.The date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (b)(1)(E)(iii), (5), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 116–283Jan. 1, 2021The Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020, referred to in subsecs. (c)(11)(A) and (i)(1), is div. F of , , . Section 6003 of the Act is set out as a note under . Such section 6003 defines terms, including the Bank Secrecy Act, as used in div. F of . Section 6502(c) of the Act is , , , which is not classified to the Code. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 2021 Amendment note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 116–283134 Stat. 4547section 5311 of this titlePub. L. 116–283section 6502(c) of title LXV of div. F of Pub. L. 116–283134 Stat. 4627section 5301 of this titleJan. 1, 2021Jan. 1, 2021

Sense of Congress, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–283, div. F, title LXIV, § 6402134 Stat. 4604Jan. 1, 2021“It is the sense of Congress that—“(1)more than 2,000,000 corporations and limited liability companies are being formed under the laws of the States each year;“(2)most or all States do not require information about the beneficial owners of the corporations, limited liability companies, or other similar entities formed under the laws of the State;“(3)malign actors seek to conceal their ownership of corporations, limited liability companies, or other similar entities in the United States to facilitate illicit activity, including money laundering, the financing of terrorism, proliferation financing, serious tax fraud, human and drug trafficking, counterfeiting, piracy, securities fraud, financial fraud, and acts of foreign corruption, harming the national security interests of the United States and allies of the United States;“(4)money launderers and others involved in commercial activity intentionally conduct transactions through corporate structures in order to evade detection, and may layer such structures, much like Russian nesting ‘Matryoshka’ dolls, across various secretive jurisdictions such that each time an investigator obtains ownership records for a domestic or foreign entity, the newly identified entity is yet another corporate entity, necessitating a repeat of the same process;“(5)Federal legislation providing for the collection of beneficial ownership information for corporations, limited liability companies, or other similar entities formed under the laws of the States is needed to—“(A)set a clear, Federal standard for incorporation practices;“(B)protect vital Unites States national security interests;“(C)protect interstate and foreign commerce;“(D)better enable critical national security, intelligence, and law enforcement efforts to counter money laundering, the financing of terrorism, and other illicit activity; and“(E)bring the United States into compliance with international anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism standards;“(6)beneficial ownership information collected under the amendments made by this title is sensitive information and will be directly available only to authorized government authorities, subject to effective safeguards and controls, to—“(A)facilitate important national security, intelligence, and law enforcement activities; and“(B)confirm beneficial ownership information provided to financial institutions to facilitate the compliance of the financial institutions with anti-money laundering, countering the financing of terrorism, and customer due diligence requirements under applicable law;“(7)consistent with applicable law, the Secretary of the Treasury shall—“(A)maintain the information described in paragraph (1) in a secure, nonpublic database, using information security methods and techniques that are appropriate to protect nonclassified information systems at the highest security level; and“(B)take all steps, including regular auditing, to ensure that government authorities accessing beneficial ownership information do so only for authorized purposes consistent with this title; and“(8)in prescribing regulations to provide for the reporting of beneficial ownership information, the Secretary shall, to the greatest extent practicable consistent with the purposes of this title—“(A)seek to minimize burdens on reporting companies associated with the collection of beneficial ownership information;“(B)provide clarity to reporting companies concerning the identification of their beneficial owners; and“(C)collect information in a form and manner that is reasonably designed to generate a database that is highly useful to national security, intelligence, and law enforcement agencies and Federal functional regulators.”[For definition of “Federal functional regulator” as used in , set out above, see , set out as a Definitions note under .]section 6402 of Pub. L. 116–283section 6003 of Pub. L. 116–283section 5311 of this title

Reporting Requirements for Federal Contractors, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–283, div. F, title LXIV, § 6403(c)134 Stat. 4623Jan. 1, 2021“(1).—In generalNot later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy shall revise the Federal Acquisition Regulation maintained under , United States Code, to require any contractor or subcontractor that is subject to the requirement to disclose beneficial ownership information under , United States Code, as added by subsection (a) of this section, to provide the information required to be disclosed under such section to the Federal Government as part of any bid or proposal for a contract with a value threshold in excess of the simplified acquisition threshold under , United States Code.Jan. 1, 2021section 1303(a)(1) of title 41section 5336 of title 31section 134 of title 41“(2).—ApplicabilityThe revision required under paragraph (1) shall not apply to a covered contractor or subcontractor, as defined in section 847[(a)(3)] of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2020 () [ note], that is subject to the beneficial ownership disclosure and review requirements under that section.”Public Law 116–9210 U.S.C. 2509

children.label: A
children.text: a nonexpired passport issued by the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: a nonexpired identification document issued by a State, local government, or Indian Tribe to the individual acting for the purpose of identification of that individual;

children.label: C
children.text: a nonexpired driver’s license issued by a State; or

children.label: D
children.text: if the individual does not have a document described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C), a nonexpired passport issued by a foreign government.

children.label: A
children.text: files an application to form a corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity under the laws of a State or Indian Tribe; or

children.label: B
children.text: registers or files an application to register a corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity formed under the laws of a foreign country to do business in the United States by filing a document with the secretary of state or similar office under the laws of a State or Indian Tribe.

children.label: i
children.text: exercises substantial control over the entity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: owns or controls not less than 25 percent of the ownership interests of the entity; and

children.label: i
children.text: a minor child, as defined in the State in which the entity is formed, if the information of the parent or guardian of the minor child is reported in accordance with this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: an individual acting as a nominee, intermediary, custodian, or agent on behalf of another individual;

children.label: iii
children.text: an individual acting solely as an employee of a corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity and whose control over or economic benefits from such entity is derived solely from the employment status of the person;

children.label: iv
children.text: an individual whose only interest in a corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity is through a right of inheritance; or

children.label: v
children.text: a creditor of a corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity, unless the creditor meets the requirements of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Director
children.label: 4 .—Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of FinCEN.

children.heading: .—FinCEN
children.label: 5 .—FinCEN
children.text: The term “FinCEN” means the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network of the Department of the Treasury.

children.heading: .—FinCEN identifier
children.label: 6 .—FinCEN identifier
children.text: The term “FinCEN identifier” means the unique identifying number assigned by FinCEN to a person under this section.

children.heading: .—Foreign person
children.label: 7 .—Foreign person
children.text: The term “foreign person” means a person who is not a United States person, as defined in section 7701(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

children.heading: .—Indian tribe
children.label: 8 .—Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given the term “Indian tribe” in section 102 of the Federally Recognized Indian Tribe List Act of 1994 ().25 U.S.C. 5130

children.heading: .—Lawfully admitted for permanent residence
children.label: 9 .—Lawfully admitted for permanent residence
children.text: The term “lawfully admitted for permanent residence” has the meaning given the term in section 101(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act ().8 U.S.C. 1101(a)

children.label: A
children.text: any investment company, as defined in section 3(a) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 (); or15 U.S.C. 80a–3(a)

children.label: i
children.text: would be an investment company under that section but for the exclusion provided from that definition by paragraph (1) or (7) of section 3(c) of that Act (); and15 U.S.C. 80a–3(c)

children.label: ii
children.text: is identified by its legal name by the applicable investment adviser in its Form ADV (or successor form) filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

children.label: i
children.text: created by the filing of a document with a secretary of state or a similar office under the law of a State or Indian Tribe; or

children.label: ii
children.text: formed under the law of a foreign country and registered to do business in the United States by the filing of a document with a secretary of state or a similar office under the laws of a State or Indian Tribe; and

children.label: I
children.text: of a class of securities registered under section 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (); or15 U.S.C. 78l

children.label: II
children.text: that is required to file supplementary and periodic information under section 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ((d));15 U.S.C. 78o

children.label: I
children.text: established under the laws of the United States, an Indian Tribe, a State, or a political subdivision of a State, or under an interstate compact between 2 or more States; and

children.label: II
children.text: that exercises governmental authority on behalf of the United States or any such Indian Tribe, State, or political subdivision;

children.label: I
children.text: section 3 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act ();12 U.S.C. 1813

children.label: II
children.text: section 2(a) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 (); or15 U.S.C. 80a–2(a)

children.label: III
children.text: section 202(a) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ();15 U.S.C. 80b–2(a)

children.label: iv
children.text: a Federal credit union or a State credit union (as those terms are defined in section 101 of the Federal Credit Union Act ());12 U.S.C. 1752

children.label: v
children.text: a bank holding company (as defined in section 2 of the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 ()) or a savings and loan holding company (as defined in section 10(a) of the Home Owners’ Loan Act ());12 U.S.C. 184112 U.S.C. 1467a(a)

children.label: vi
children.text: a money transmitting business registered with the Secretary of the Treasury under section 5330;

children.label: vii
children.text: a broker or dealer (as those terms are defined in section 3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ()) that is registered under section 15 of that Act ();15 U.S.C. 78c15 U.S.C. 78o

children.label: viii
children.text: an exchange or clearing agency (as those terms are defined in section 3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ()) that is registered under section 6 or 17A of that Act (, 78q–1);15 U.S.C. 78c15 U.S.C. 78f

children.label: ix
children.text: any other entity not described in clause (i), (vii), or (viii) that is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 78a

children.label: I
children.text: is an investment company (as defined in section 3 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 ()) or an investment adviser (as defined in section 202 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ()); and15 U.S.C. 80a–315 U.S.C. 80b–2

children.label: II
children.text: is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( et seq.) or the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ( et seq.);15 U.S.C. 80a–115 U.S.C. 80b–1

children.label: I
children.text: described in section 203() of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 (()); andll15 U.S.C. 80b–3

children.label: II
children.text: that has filed Item 10, Schedule A, and Schedule B of Part 1A of Form ADV, or any successor thereto, with the Securities and Exchange Commission;

children.label: xii
children.text: an insurance company (as defined in section 2 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 ());15 U.S.C. 80a–2

children.label: I
children.text: is an insurance producer that is authorized by a State and subject to supervision by the insurance commissioner or a similar official or agency of a State; and

children.label: II
children.text: has an operating presence at a physical office within the United States;

children.label: I
children.text: a registered entity (as defined in section 1a of the Commodity Exchange Act ()); or7 U.S.C. 1a

children.label: AA
children.text: a futures commission merchant, introducing broker, swap dealer, major swap participant, commodity pool operator, or commodity trading advisor (as those terms are defined in section 1a of the Commodity Exchange Act ()); or7 U.S.C. 1a

children.label: BB
children.text: a retail foreign exchange dealer, as described in section 2(c)(2)(B) of that Act (); and7 U.S.C. 2(c)(2)(B)

children.label: bb
children.text: registered with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission under the Commodity Exchange Act ( et seq.);7 U.S.C. 1

children.label: xv
children.text: a public accounting firm registered in accordance with section 102 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 ();15 U.S.C. 7212

children.label: xvi
children.text: a public utility that provides telecommunications services, electrical power, natural gas, or water and sewer services within the United States;

children.label: xvii
children.text: a financial market utility designated by the Financial Stability Oversight Council under section 804 of the Payment, Clearing, and Settlement Supervision Act of 2010 ();12 U.S.C. 5463

children.label: xviii
children.text: any pooled investment vehicle that is operated or advised by a person described in clause (iii), (iv), (vii), (x), or (xi);

children.label: I
children.text: organization that is described in section 501(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (determined without regard to section 508(a) of such Code) and exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such Code, except that in the case of any such organization that loses an exemption from tax, such organization shall be considered to be continued to be described in this subclause for the 180-day period beginning on the date of the loss of such tax-exempt status;

children.label: II
children.text: political organization (as defined in section 527(e)(1) of such Code) that is exempt from tax under section 527(a) of such Code; or

children.label: III
children.text: trust described in paragraph (1) or (2) of section 4947(a) of such Code;

children.label: I
children.text: operates exclusively to provide financial assistance to, or hold governance rights over, any entity described in clause (xix);

children.label: II
children.text: is a United States person;

children.label: III
children.text: is beneficially owned or controlled exclusively by 1 or more United States persons that are United States citizens or lawfully admitted for permanent residence; and

children.label: IV
children.text: derives at least a majority of its funding or revenue from 1 or more United States persons that are United States citizens or lawfully admitted for permanent residence;

children.label: I
children.text: employs more than 20 employees on a full-time basis in the United States;

children.label: aa
children.text: other entities owned by the entity; and

children.label: bb
children.text: other entities through which the entity operates; and

children.label: III
children.text: has an operating presence at a physical office within the United States;

children.label: xxii
children.text: any corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity of which the ownership interests are owned or controlled, directly or indirectly, by 1 or more entities described in clause (i), (ii), (iii), (iv), (v), (vii), (viii), (ix), (x), (xi), (xii), (xiii), (xiv), (xv), (xvi), (xvii) (xix), or (xxi);1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.label: I
children.text: in existence for over 1 year;

children.label: II
children.text: that is not engaged in active business;

children.label: III
children.text: that is not owned, directly or indirectly, by a foreign person;

children.label: IV
children.text: that has not, in the preceding 12-month period, experienced a change in ownership or sent or received funds in an amount greater than $1,000 (including all funds sent to or received from any source through a financial account or accounts in which the entity, or an affiliate of the entity, maintains an interest); and

children.label: V
children.text: that does not otherwise hold any kind or type of assets, including an ownership interest in any corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity;

children.label: I
children.text: would not serve the public interest; and

children.label: II
children.text: would not be highly useful in national security, intelligence, and law enforcement agency efforts to detect, prevent, or prosecute money laundering, the financing of terrorism, proliferation finance, serious tax fraud, or other crimes.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 12 .—State
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, American Samoa, Guam, the United States Virgin Islands, and any other commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.

children.heading: .—Unique identifying number
children.label: 13 .—Unique identifying number
children.text: The term “unique identifying number” means, with respect to an individual or an entity with a sole member, the unique identifying number from an acceptable identification document.

children.heading: .—United states person
children.label: 14 .—United states person
children.text: The term “United States person” has the meaning given the term in section 7701(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: In accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, each reporting company shall submit to FinCEN a report that contains the information described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Reporting of existing entities
children.label: B .—Reporting of existing entities
children.text: In accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, any reporting company that has been formed or registered before the effective date of the regulations prescribed under this subsection shall, in a timely manner, and not later than 2 years after the effective date of the regulations prescribed under this subsection, submit to FinCEN a report that contains the information described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Reporting at time of formation or registration
children.label: C .—Reporting at time of formation or registration
children.text: In accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, any reporting company that has been formed or registered after the effective date of the regulations promulgated under this subsection shall, at the time of formation or registration, submit to FinCEN a report that contains the information described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Updated reporting for changes in beneficial ownership
children.label: D .—Updated reporting for changes in beneficial ownership
children.text: In accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, a reporting company shall, in a timely manner, and not later than 1 year after the date on which there is a change with respect to any information described in paragraph (2), submit to FinCEN a report that updates the information relating to the change.

children.heading: .—Treasury review of updated reporting for changes in beneficial ownership
children.label: E .—Treasury review of updated reporting for changes in beneficial ownership
i
children.text: the necessity of a requirement for corporations, limited liability companies, or other similar entities to update the report on beneficial ownership information in paragraph (2), related to a change in ownership, within a shorter period of time than required under subparagraph (D), taking into account the updating requirements under subparagraph (D) and the information contained in the reports;

children.label: ii
children.text: the benefit to law enforcement and national security officials that might be derived from, and the burden that a requirement to update the list of beneficial owners within a shorter period of time after a change in the list of beneficial owners would impose on corporations, limited liability companies, or other similar entities; and2So in original.2

children.label: iii
children.text: not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of this section, incorporate into the regulations, as appropriate, any changes necessary to implement the findings and determinations based on the review required under this subparagraph.2

children.label: i
children.text: establish partnerships with State, local, and Tribal governmental agencies;

children.label: ii
children.text: collect information described in paragraph (2) through existing Federal, State, and local processes and procedures;

children.label: iii
children.text: minimize burdens on reporting companies associated with the collection of the information described in paragraph (2), in light of the private compliance costs placed on legitimate businesses, including by identifying any steps taken to mitigate the costs relating to compliance with the collection of information; and

children.label: I
children.text: facilitating important national security, intelligence, and law enforcement activities; and

children.label: II
children.text: confirming beneficial ownership information provided to financial institutions to facilitate the compliance of the financial institutions with anti-money laundering, countering the financing of terrorism, and customer due diligence requirements under applicable law.

children.heading: .—Regulatory simplification
children.label: G .—Regulatory simplification
children.text: To simplify compliance with this section for reporting companies and financial institutions, the Secretary of the Treasury shall ensure that the regulations prescribed by the Secretary under this subsection are added to part 1010 of title 31, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor thereto.

children.label: i
children.text: full legal name;

children.label: ii
children.text: date of birth;

children.label: iii
children.text: current, as of the date on which the report is delivered, residential or business street address; and

children.label: I
children.text: unique identifying number from an acceptable identification document; or

children.label: II
children.text: FinCEN identifier in accordance with requirements in paragraph (3).

children.heading: .—Reporting requirement for exempt entities having an ownership interest
children.label: B .—Reporting requirement for exempt entities having an ownership interest
i
children.text: shall, with respect to the exempt entity, only list the name of the exempt entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not be required to report the information with respect to the exempt entity otherwise required under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Reporting requirement for certain pooled investment vehicles
children.label: C .—Reporting requirement for certain pooled investment vehicles
children.text: Any corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity that is an exempt entity described in subsection (a)(11)(B)(xviii) and is formed under the laws of a foreign country shall file with FinCEN a written certification that provides identification information of an individual that exercises substantial control over the pooled investment vehicle in the same manner as required under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Reporting requirement for exempt subsidiaries
children.label: D .—Reporting requirement for exempt subsidiaries
children.text: In accordance with the regulations promulgated by the Secretary, any corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity that is an exempt entity described in subsection (a)(11)(B)(xxii), shall, at the time such entity no longer meets the criteria described in subsection (a)(11)(B)(xxii), submit to FinCEN a report containing the information required under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Reporting requirement for exempt grandfathered entities
children.label: E .—Reporting requirement for exempt grandfathered entities
children.text: In accordance with the regulations promulgated by the Secretary, any corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity that is an exempt entity described in subsection (a)(11)(B)(xxiii), shall, at the time such entity no longer meets the criteria described in subsection (a)(11)(B)(xxiii), submit to FinCEN a report containing the information required under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Upon request by an individual who has provided FinCEN with the information described in paragraph (2)(A) pertaining to the individual, or by an entity that has reported its beneficial ownership information to FinCEN in accordance with this section, FinCEN shall issue a FinCEN identifier to such individual or entity.

children.heading: .—Updating of information
children.label: ii .—Updating of information
children.text: An individual or entity with a FinCEN identifier shall submit filings with FinCEN pursuant to paragraph (1) updating any information described in paragraph (2) in a timely manner consistent with paragraph (1)(D).

children.heading: .—Exclusive identifier
children.label: iii .—Exclusive identifier
children.text: FinCEN shall not issue more than 1 FinCEN identifier to the same individual or to the same entity (including any successor entity).

children.heading: .—Use of fincen identifier for individuals
children.label: B .—Use of fincen identifier for individuals
children.text: Any person required to report the information described in paragraph (2) with respect to an individual may instead report the FinCEN identifier of the individual.

children.heading: .—Use of fincen identifier for entities
children.label: C .—Use of fincen identifier for entities
children.text: If an individual is or may be a beneficial owner of a reporting company by an interest held by the individual in an entity that, directly or indirectly, holds an interest in the reporting company, the reporting company may report the FinCEN identifier of the entity in lieu of providing the information required by paragraph (2)(A) with respect to the individual.

children.label: A
children.text: by regulation prescribe procedures and standards governing any report under paragraph (2) and any FinCEN identifier under paragraph (3); and

children.label: i
children.text: minimize burdens on reporting companies associated with the collection of beneficial ownership information, including by eliminating duplicative requirements; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure the beneficial ownership information reported to FinCEN is accurate, complete, and highly useful.

children.heading: .—Effective date
children.label: 5 .—Effective date
children.text: The requirements of this subsection shall take effect on the effective date of the regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury under this subsection, which shall be promulgated not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the effectiveness of those procedures and standards in minimizing reporting burdens (including through the elimination of duplicative requirements) and strengthening the accuracy of reports submitted under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: any alternative procedures and standards prescribed to carry out paragraph (2).

children.heading: Retention and Disclosure of Beneficial Ownership Information by FinCEN.—
children.label: c Retention and Disclosure of Beneficial Ownership Information by FinCEN.—

children.heading: .—Retention of information
children.label: 1 .—Retention of information
children.text: Beneficial ownership information required under subsection (b) relating to each reporting company shall be maintained by FinCEN for not fewer than 5 years after the date on which the reporting company terminates.

children.label: i
children.text: an officer or employee of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: an officer or employee of any State, local, or Tribal agency; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an officer or employee of any financial institution or regulatory agency receiving information under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: from a Federal agency engaged in national security, intelligence, or law enforcement activity, for use in furtherance of such activity; or

children.label: II
children.text: from a State, local, or Tribal law enforcement agency, if a court of competent jurisdiction, including any officer of such a court, has authorized the law enforcement agency to seek the information in a criminal or civil investigation;

children.label: I
children.text: issued in response to a request for assistance in an investigation or prosecution by such foreign country; and

children.label: aa
children.text: requires compliance with the disclosure and use provisions of the treaty, agreement, or convention, publicly disclosing any beneficial ownership information received; or

children.label: bb
children.text: limits the use of the information for any purpose other than the authorized investigation or national security or intelligence activity;

children.label: iii
children.text: a request made by a financial institution subject to customer due diligence requirements, with the consent of the reporting company, to facilitate the compliance of the financial institution with customer due diligence requirements under applicable law; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a request made by a Federal functional regulator or other appropriate regulatory agency consistent with the requirements of subparagraph (C).

children.heading: .—Form and manner of disclosure to financial institutions and regulatory agencies
children.label: C .—Form and manner of disclosure to financial institutions and regulatory agencies
i
children.text: is authorized by law to assess, supervise, enforce, or otherwise determine the compliance of the financial institution with the requirements described in that subparagraph;

children.label: ii
children.text: uses the information solely for the purpose of conducting the assessment, supervision, or authorized investigation or activity described in clause (i); and

children.label: iii
children.text: enters into an agreement with the Secretary providing for appropriate protocols governing the safekeeping of the information.

children.label: A
children.text: protect the security and confidentiality of any beneficial ownership information provided directly by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: require the head of any requesting agency, on a non-delegable basis, to approve the standards and procedures utilized by the requesting agency and certify to the Secretary semi-annually that such standards and procedures are in compliance with the requirements of this paragraph;

children.label: C
children.text: require the requesting agency to establish and maintain, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, a secure system in which such beneficial ownership information provided directly by the Secretary shall be stored;

children.label: D
children.text: require the requesting agency to furnish a report to the Secretary, at such time and containing such information as the Secretary may prescribe, that describes the procedures established and utilized by such agency to ensure the confidentiality of the beneficial ownership information provided directly by the Secretary;

children.label: i
children.text: states that applicable requirements have been met, in such form and manner as the Secretary may prescribe; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at a minimum, sets forth the specific reason or reasons why the beneficial ownership information is relevant to an authorized investigation or other activity described in paragraph (2);

children.label: F
children.text: require the requesting agency to limit, to the greatest extent practicable, the scope of information sought, consistent with the purposes for seeking beneficial ownership information;

children.label: i
children.text: who are directly engaged in the authorized investigation or activity described in paragraph (2);

children.label: ii
children.text: whose duties or responsibilities require such access;

children.label: I
children.text: have undergone appropriate training; or

children.label: II
children.text: use staff to access the database who have undergone appropriate training;

children.label: iv
children.text: who use appropriate identity verification mechanisms to obtain access to the information; and

children.label: v
children.text: who are authorized by agreement with the Secretary to access the information;

children.label: H
children.text: require the requesting agency to establish and maintain, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, a permanent system of standardized records with respect to an auditable trail of each request for beneficial ownership information submitted to the Secretary by the agency, including the reason for the request, the name of the individual who made the request, the date of the request, any disclosure of beneficial ownership information made by or to the agency, and any other information the Secretary of the Treasury determines is appropriate;

children.label: I
children.text: require that the requesting agency receiving beneficial ownership information from the Secretary conduct an annual audit to verify that the beneficial ownership information received from the Secretary has been accessed and used appropriately, and in a manner consistent with this paragraph and provide the results of that audit to the Secretary upon request;

children.label: J
children.text: require the Secretary to conduct an annual audit of the adherence of the agencies to the protocols established under this paragraph to ensure that agencies are requesting and using beneficial ownership information appropriately; and

children.label: K
children.text: provide such other safeguards which the Secretary determines (and which the Secretary prescribes in regulations) to be necessary or appropriate to protect the confidentiality of the beneficial ownership information.

children.heading: .—Violation of protocols
children.label: 4 .—Violation of protocols
children.text: Any employee or officer of a requesting agency under paragraph (2)(B) that violates the protocols described in paragraph (3), including unauthorized disclosure or use, shall be subject to criminal and civil penalties under subsection (h)(3)(B).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Beneficial ownership information shall be accessible for inspection or disclosure to officers and employees of the Department of the Treasury whose official duties require such inspection or disclosure subject to procedures and safeguards prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: .—Tax administration purposes
children.label: B .—Tax administration purposes
children.text: Officers and employees of the Department of the Treasury may obtain access to beneficial ownership information for tax administration purposes in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: shall reject a request not submitted in the form and manner prescribed by the Secretary under paragraph (2)(C); and

children.label: i
children.text: the requesting agency has failed to meet any other requirement of this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: the information is being requested for an unlawful purpose; or

children.label: iii
children.text: other good cause exists to deny the request.

children.heading: .—Suspension
children.label: 7 .—Suspension
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may suspend or debar a requesting agency from access for any of the grounds set forth in paragraph (6), including for repeated or serious violations of any requirement under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: are consistent with standards and guidelines developed under subchapter II of chapter 35 of title 44; and

children.label: B
children.text: incorporate Federal information system security controls for high-impact systems, excluding national security systems, consistent with applicable law to prevent the loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability of information that may have a severe or catastrophic adverse effect.

children.label: A
children.text: may include a classified annex; and

children.label: i
children.text: the date on which the request was submitted;

children.label: ii
children.text: the source of the request;

children.label: iii
children.text: whether the request was accepted or rejected or is pending; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a general description of the basis for rejecting the such request, if applicable.

children.label: A
children.text: audit the procedures and safeguards established by the Secretary of the Treasury under those regulations, including duties for verification of requesting agencies systems and adherence to the protocols established under this subsection, to determine whether such safeguards and procedures meet the requirements of this subsection and that the Department of the Treasury is using beneficial ownership information appropriately in a manner consistent with this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit to the Secretary of the Treasury, the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate, and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives a report that contains the findings and determinations with respect to any audit conducted under this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: anticipated plans, goals, and resources necessary for operations of FinCEN in implementing the requirements of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020 and the amendments made by that Act;

children.label: I
children.text: ensure that the requirements and obligations imposed upon FinCEN by the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020 and the amendments made by that Act are completed as efficiently, effectively, and expeditiously as possible; and

children.label: II
children.text: provide for robust and effective implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020 and the amendments made by that Act;

children.label: iii
children.text: strengthen FinCEN management efforts, as necessary and as identified by the Director, to meet the requirements of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020 and the amendments made by that Act;2

children.label: I
children.text: educating the business community on the goals and operations of the new beneficial ownership database; and

children.label: II
children.text: disseminating to the governments of countries that are allies or partners of the United States information on best practices developed by FinCEN related to beneficial ownership information retention and use;

children.label: v
children.text: any policy recommendations that could facilitate and improve communication and coordination between the private sector, FinCEN, and the Federal, State, and local agencies and entities involved in implementing innovative approaches to meet their obligations under the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020 and the amendments made by that Act, the Bank Secrecy Act (as defined in section 6003 of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020), and other anti-money laundering compliance laws; and

children.label: vi
children.text: any other matter that the Director determines is appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be submitted in unclassified form; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may include a classified portion.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall, to the greatest extent practicable, update the information described in subsection (b) by working collaboratively with other relevant Federal, State, and Tribal agencies.

children.heading: .—Information from relevant federal, state, and tribal agencies
children.label: 2 .—Information from relevant federal, state, and tribal agencies
children.text: Relevant Federal, State, and Tribal agencies, as determined by the Secretary of the Treasury, shall, to the extent practicable, and consistent with applicable legal protections, cooperate with and provide information requested by FinCEN for purposes of maintaining an accurate, complete, and highly useful database for beneficial ownership information.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: 3 .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury, in consultation with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies, may promulgate regulations as necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: .—Federal
children.label: 1 .—Federal
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall take reasonable steps to provide notice to persons of their obligations to report beneficial ownership information under this section, including by causing appropriate informational materials describing such obligations to be included in 1 or more forms or other informational materials regularly distributed by the Internal Revenue Service and FinCEN.

children.label: I
children.text: notify filers of their requirements as reporting companies under this section, including the requirements to file and update reports under paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (b); and

children.label: II
children.text: provide the filers with a copy of the reporting company form created by the Secretary of the Treasury under this subsection or an internet link to that form.

children.label: ii
children.text: The secretary of a State or a similar office in each State or Indian Tribe responsible for the formation or registration of entities created by the filing of a public document with the office under the law of the State or Indian Tribes shall update the websites, forms relating to incorporation, and physical premises of the office to notify filers of their requirements as reporting companies under this section, including providing an internet link to the reporting company form created by the Secretary of the Treasury under this section.

children.heading: .—Notification from the department of the treasury
children.label: B .—Notification from the department of the treasury
children.text: A notification under clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A) shall explicitly state that the notification is on behalf of the Department of the Treasury for the purpose of preventing money laundering, the financing of terrorism, proliferation financing, serious tax fraud, and other financial crime by requiring nonpublic registration of business entities formed or registered to do business in the United States.

children.heading: .—No Bearer Share Corporations or Limited Liability Companies
children.label: f .—No Bearer Share Corporations or Limited Liability Companies
children.text: A corporation, limited liability company, or other similar entity formed under the laws of a State or Indian Tribe may not issue a certificate in bearer form evidencing either a whole or fractional interest in the entity.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: g .—Regulations
children.text: In promulgating regulations carrying out this section, the Director shall reach out to members of the small business community and other appropriate parties to ensure efficiency and effectiveness of the process for the entities subject to the requirements of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: willfully provide, or attempt to provide, false or fraudulent beneficial ownership information, including a false or fraudulent identifying photograph or document, to FinCEN in accordance with subsection (b); or

children.label: B
children.text: willfully fail to report complete or updated beneficial ownership information to FinCEN in accordance with subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: a report submitted to FinCEN under subsection (b); or

children.label: B
children.text: a disclosure made by FinCEN under subsection (c).

children.label: i
children.text: shall be liable to the United States for a civil penalty of not more than $500 for each day that the violation continues or has not been remedied; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may be fined not more than $10,000, imprisoned for not more than 2 years, or both.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be liable to the United States for a civil penalty of not more than $500 for each day that the violation continues or has not been remedied; and

children.label: I
children.text: shall be fined not more than $250,000, or imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both; or

children.label: II
children.text: while violating another law of the United States or as part of a pattern of any illegal activity involving more than $100,000 in a 12-month period, shall be fined not more than $500,000, imprisoned for not more than 10 years, or both.

children.label: aa
children.text: has reason to believe that any report submitted by the person in accordance with subsection (b) contains inaccurate information; and

children.label: bb
children.text: in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary, voluntarily and promptly, and in no case later than 90 days after the date on which the person submitted the report, submits a report containing corrected information.

children.label: aa
children.text: acts for the purpose of evading the reporting requirements under subsection (b); and

children.label: bb
children.text: has actual knowledge that any information contained in the report is inaccurate.

children.heading: .—Assistance
children.label: ii .—Assistance
children.text: FinCEN shall provide assistance to any person seeking to submit a corrected report in accordance with clause (i)(I).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury, in coordination with the Secretary of the Treasury, shall provide public contact information to receive external comments or complaints regarding the beneficial ownership information notification and collection process or regarding the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of such information.

children.label: i
children.text: summarizes external comments or complaints and related investigations conducted by the Inspector General related to the collection of beneficial ownership information; and

children.label: ii
children.text: includes recommendations, in coordination with FinCEN, to improve the form and manner of the notification, collection and updating processes of the beneficial ownership information reporting requirements to ensure the beneficial ownership information reported to FinCEN is accurate, complete, and highly useful.

children.heading: Treasury office of inspector general investigation in the event of a cybersecurity breach.—
children.label: 5 Treasury office of inspector general investigation in the event of a cybersecurity breach.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: In the event of a cybersecurity breach that results in substantial unauthorized access and disclosure of sensitive beneficial ownership information, the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall conduct an investigation into FinCEN cybersecurity practices that, to the extent possible, determines any vulnerabilities within FinCEN information security and confidentiality protocols and provides recommendations for fixing those deficiencies.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: B .—Report
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall submit to the Secretary of the Treasury a report on each investigation conducted under subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: determine whether the Director had any responsibility for the cybersecurity breach or whether policies, practices, or procedures implemented at the direction of the Director led to the cybersecurity breach; and

children.label: ii
children.text: submit to Congress a written report outlining the findings of the Secretary, including a determination by the Secretary on whether to retain or dismiss the individual serving as the Director.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: 6 .—Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “willfully” means the voluntary, intentional violation of a known legal duty.

children.heading: Continuous Review of Exempt Entities.—
children.label: i Continuous Review of Exempt Entities.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: On and after the effective date of the regulations promulgated under subsection (b)(4), if the Secretary of the Treasury makes a determination, which may be based on information contained in the report required under section 6502(c) of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020 or on any other information available to the Secretary, that an entity or class of entities described in subsection (a)(11)(B) has been involved in significant abuse relating to money laundering, the financing of terrorism, proliferation finance, serious tax fraud, or any other financial crime, not later than 90 days after the date on which the Secretary makes the determination, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives a report that explains the reasons for the determination and any administrative or legislative recommendations to prevent such abuse.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be submitted in unclassified form; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include a classified annex.

children.heading: .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.label: j .—Authorization of Appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to FinCEN for each of the 3 fiscal years beginning on the effective date of the regulations promulgated under subsection (b)(4), such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section, including allocating funds to the States to pay reasonable costs relating to compliance with the requirements of such section.
